note.text: Formally confirmed at follow-up encounter on 2025-07-23. Meets revised Sapporo (Sydney) classification criteria: clinical criterion (three consecutive unexplained early pregnancy losses before 10 weeks gestation) combined with laboratory criteria (persistently positive anticardiolipin IgG at moderate titer 52 GPL, persistently positive lupus anticoagulant, now-positive anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I IgG at 22 units confirmed at >12 weeks). Risk stratification: medium risk. Patient is not triple-positive at high titer, which is a favorable prognostic factor. Thrombophilia screening entirely negative — no concurrent inherited thrombophilia.
